chunks.text: In a democratic nation, to elect is the most fundamental act.
Citizens regularly go to the polls and choose their representative.
When the campaign begins, each candidate puts forward an agenda and appeals to the voters.
Healthcare, education, the economy, the environment.
Which issue to emphasize becomes the mark of each candidate.
Sometimes the election does not give a single party the majority.
In that case, several parties form a coalition and share power.
In a coalition government, compromise is essential.
The elected leader appoints cabinet members and leads each department of the federal government.
A new bill becomes law only after receiving approval from the assembly.
In recent years, interest in elections has declined in some countries.
Turnout falls, and some young people ignore politics, saying
" It has nothing to do with me.
" But those who do not vote are not entitled to complain about the result.
Democracy functions only through the participation of its citizens.
Meanwhile, there are countries in the world where elections are not even held.
Advocates continue to call for the right of every person to vote.
Elections are not perfect, but they remain one of the most crucial tools we have.
